author: Louise S. H.
pros: We have used Timelog at IIH Nordic for several years and the system works well to keep track of time registrations, finances & projects. New features are constantly being added and the timelog team is also very responsive to users' wishes and needs in relation to future releases. The custumer success teams is always very helpful and responds quickly to inquiries.
cons: There are many reports in the system - however not all of them easy to navigate.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Ease of use. Good integration with Jira.
description: we use this for time and finance management AFAIK. Checking overtime and registering vacation time.
author: Elisabeth H.
pros: Easy to track and manage timelogging that is outside of our jira scope. Easy to use when you have holidays and vacations to track. It also makes it possible to see accumulated overtime.
cons: I wish there was an option to see accumulated overtime without having to load the calendar for several months back.

review_title: Good tool for basic time tracking
description: We use TimeLog to track staff time by projects. We then pull the data out into Excel where we process the information and generate our invoices each month. It works OK, but is a bit cumbersome. The forecasting/project planning features seem relevant and useful, but we can't make them work for our purposes. If TimeLog spent more effort in usability and context-specific help descriptions, it would vastly improve the usability of the system for us.
author: Mikkel N.
pros: The app works great for time tracking project by project. Easy to deploy to new users. Great and fast customer support. Great up-time (very rare that the app is not working).
cons: The product is very ambitious about the many features and integrations it offers, but the usability of all but the most basic features is very complex and requires substantial IT skills to make work. For our business, the required effort is too much, so we just use a fraction of the features. This makes the product more expensive for us than it should be. For instance, the system for hourly rates is a maze to navigate, and once a project is created it is impossible to shift to another rate card - even if the first chosen rate card was a mistake. Frustrating.

author: Palle H.
pros: I like the setup of project and contracts and how this gives me a lot of flexibility in the way we tailor invoices etc to customers
cons: - When you generate and invoice the details lines comes in a random order and not sorted by date. - Some reports can only be done on week numbers not specifik dates and others the other way around. - It always clear what hourly rate is used. Eg when I set and hourly rate on the project and on the task, then it would be nice to see the price that is used for the resources allocated to the task and not just "not specific price specified"

description: Saves a lot of time for managing projects. But, the invoice should be better. There are a few problems which needs to be solved. Apart from that, its a good product to have.
author: Sumon H.
pros: The reporting about individuals is great.
cons: The customer support is the worst. Have to leave a message at zendesk. Then they will reply on their convenient time. As we are paying pro level price, so we demand pro level customer service. Not enough customisation option. No notification system on milestones. The gantt charts is not very well thought. It should be better in design.

author: Elian J.
pros: I really like the entry level costs as they were when we entered several years ago. I have no clue if it still offer the same entry level.Support has always been fast to reply and the operator has always been able to either answer by themselves or request immediate help from back office.
cons: The entry level subsribtion has an odd limitation: currency. You can only use 1 currency at entry level. In other words, TimeLog thinks that the fact that you do business with companies that runs on a different currency or are operating from abroad, means that our company must be wealthy.

review_title: TimeLog is great for Time&Material business'
description: As a consultancy company we live from billing time/hours and TimeLogs Time-to-Money process is just what I needed, when I joined a previous consultancy company with lots of project, fine turnover and a bottom line like a nightmare. So when I started Carve Consulting in 2013, I wasn't in doubt: TimeLog was a must as the single, administrative tools for all consultants and partners. We use it for time-registration, expenses, project management etc. Even those of my colleagues who haven't great flair for administrative tasks can be helped to do the full process: From time to money. Behind the scenes TimeLog is integrated to e-conomic as our accounting system, which I'm the only one to access. Yes, Carve Consulting is a true TimeLog Company.
author: Mikael R.
pros: Ease and flexibility of use in the sense, that it allows you combine classic project/time management with the financial details of the underlying contract. In this way the work of collecting the time registrations and converting them to an invoice is doable for everybody. In our business almost every contract have some unique 'details' like pricing, in and out of scope, payment schedules, etc. TimeLog supports these 'details' which enables the Time-to-Money process to be simple. All it takes is to do the initial job of setting-up the project correctly. Garbage in, garbage out.
cons: All I miss is an app what supports expenses on-the-go: Scan recite. Done. I'm pretty sure it's on its way.

description: Overall experience has been really nice, i would highly recently if.
author: Mateen Y.
pros: The time tracking has been designed to be user friendly.
cons: Interface is simple, but sometimes it feels too outdated.

author: Birgit O.
pros: Timelog has a great usability, the majority of the functionalities are easy to find and understand and are overall self explanatory. The on-boarding process for new employees is extremely easy and can be done quickly. The billing process is also easy to learn and invoices can be issued within minutes. The time logging process and reminder functions help to keep our business going. Timelog is a very flexible system and we are able to manage and invoice even the most complicated projects in a timely manner. The fact that Timelog is web based makes work very convenient for all employees, project managers and accountants and is an important factor.
cons: Some versions of Timelog worked better than others. Since we use the system for almost 12 years , there were a few functionalities in older versions that weren't great, but with the newest version right now everything seems to be fine. Some functionalities are only useful for Scandinavian customers, but we usually find a work around.

review_title: I have worked in multiple system so far, and will definitely recommend TimeLog as my all time favori
author: Trine G.
pros: TimeLog is super easy to use and give a superb overview of both financial figures and resources available. Through TimeLog's many reporting options, it is easy to do the reporting needed by the management team. With our usage of TimeLog we are following the development of our resources closely. Their resource planer gives a perfect overview of available resources and when a resource is fully booked. From an employees point of view it is also easy to see which tasks are planned for the future. The invoicing is done flawlessly, and if you keep the system updated with the correct data along the way, it takes me a quarter of the time compared to colleagues using other systems. I would like to highlight TimeLog's support team they are always sweet, patient and extremely helpful, and they truly understand how to create an amazing customer experience!
cons: Even though we are pleased with the resource planner, it is also one of the features that could use a brush-up. It is a bit difficult to move the allocated hours between days, if you are doing a detailed planning. I know they are working on improving this, which I am looking forward to. I also miss an extension to the pipeline management.

author: Christian Møller J.
pros: registration of time and expenses is good
cons: It can not communicate with our ERP system, that means a lot of manual work ,with a risk of failure

review_title: TimeLog Projects is easy to use and gives me daily insights into hours on my customers
author: Allan L.
pros: Timelog is easy to use and gives me insights into hours spend on "my" projects and customers. Our invoicing is also done through Timelog's interface and the integration with other tools works really good. Our experience is that if the project is set up correctly the process from registration to invoice and payment is handled smoothly with integration to Economics.
cons: As a Consulting Company we had a need for further analytics and reporting and we established Accobat BI for Timelog. A standard report package for timelog users. It fits perfect together with TimeLogs open platform.

author: Mikkel S.
pros: The commercial value on the projects from the PM's perspective
cons: Reporting needs an overhaul, there are many reports but a modernization is desired.

description: Our controlling and project overview in terms of registration and invoiceing is really good. Our quailty in timeregistration has definately been improved after we started using timelog.
author: Frederik S.
pros: We use and have been using timelog for a while now. The posibilites to make our teamleders approve timeregistration and creative invoice drafts is amazing. one click invoicing is also one of the features we love a lot.
cons: Estimated hours on fixed price or recuring monthly tasks would be nice.

author: Agne V.
pros: It is easy to use and easy to learn, how to use it, plus the process from time tracking to the invoicing is very user friendly.
cons: The reporting part could be more flexible.

author: Jens M.
pros: The option to be able to put add favourite job numbers.
cons: The lookup option could be optimized and made more intuitive.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Steffen G.
pros: i like that i can easily make up the time if i forget to log in to the company in the morning
cons: since I only track daily time, I can't say anything negative about it
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Covers many needs, not intuitive though
description: Might provide help for the finance team but not really user friendly.
author: Christian R.
pros: In general time tracking and budget planning is accurate and might provide lots of insights and report functions
cons: Complexity by being not intuitive at all in using the tool. Most functions are not useful for me and my fellow colleagues.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Overall I'm very satisfied with TimeLog but the problem to export information to excel and sometimes also print reports gives you only four stars above instead of five. Export reports to Excel will do TimeLog to a five stars software.
author: LiseLott R.
pros: Easy to learn
cons: Export reports to excel doesn't work

description: TimeLog is very easy to use. It gives us a great overview of our business. We can take out different reports and review our business.
author: Lotte Boye J.
pros: The overview that the software gives.

author: Hannib-Khan B.
pros: Good way to get data on different types of projects. Many features.
cons: A bit difficult to use in the start, because of so many different features.

review_title: Good and effective control of time
author: Ebbe S.
pros: The Timelog system supplies a wide varity of usage possibilites, which goes all the way from registration of time, to control, reporting and optimization. I highly recommend it to any that sells billable hours and wish do so efficiently.
cons: Few cons with the pros taken into account. Every system has errors, but Timelog has a very customer oriented support staff, that do all they can to help as quickly as possible.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: JAMIE A.
pros: i gave it a free try and liked it easy to use
cons: it did not have all the features i needed
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

author: Rahul K.
pros: Timelog does a great job in managing projects, employee tracking, resource allocation all in one software. It is suitable for business of all sizes, small and large enterprises.
cons: TimeLog can be improved by shifting away from manual administration of timesheets. Also, a missing timesheet can lead to delayed invoicing prone to error.

description: Good for time tracking, although more features are desirable. The system is clearly not developed for consultancy firms that need to invoice time and material, disbursements, official fees and other costs in the same invoice. I think for other companies selling goods, it is a good system that works well in many ways.
author: Trine Terp J.
pros: Easy to make a single invoice, easy to track time in the time sheet, WIP overview
cons: It's very difficult to have even smaller customisations, it took nearly two years to develop a new invoice template which then still needs manual editing as some lines cannot be transferred to Economics. The specification only comes in one format and apparently it's impossible to develop a new (additional) format. You cannot change debtor on a project once a time registration has been made (would be good so you don't have to create new projects). Too many bugs appearing. After 3 years we still experience new bugs we never saw before.
